data.title: GDP Shrank Less Than First Reported Last Quarter. This Is Why. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Megan Leonhardt

The Bureau of Economic Analysis revised its estimate of first-quarter gross domestic product to an annual rate of 0.2% decline, compared with the previously reported 0.3% drop.

That marks a notable shift from the 2.4% gain in the fourth quarter, and is marginally better than the 0.3% decline expected by economists surveyed by Bloomberg and FactSet.

This is breaking news. Read a preview of the GDP data below and check back for more analysis soon.

Don't expect to see much of a change to the latest figure for U.S. economic growth when it is updated on Thursday.

Inflation-adjusted gross domestic product declined at an annual rate of 0.3% over the three months ended in March, according to last month's preliminary estimate from the Bureau of Economic Analysis. That marked a notable reversal from the fourth quarter's 2.4% gain.

But that isn't the final word on the subject. The BEA releases three estimates of quarterly GDP growth, with the second estimate due out Thursday at 8:30 a.m. Eastern. The second and third estimates incorporate more data and revised estimates compared with the first, advance estimate.

Despite the mixed results in the advance estimate's underlying data, economists don't expect the second estimate of first-quarter GDP to contain big enough revisions to move the needle substantially. The consensus forecasts from both Bloomberg and FactSet call for a decline of 0.3%.

"While there is scope for some sizable revisions, we expect the crosscurrents to still point to a small contraction last quarter," writes Michael Feroli, chief U.S. economist at J.P. Morgan.

Inventory counts may tick up, but that boost to GDP growth could be offset by downward revisions to business investment and home-building activity. Luigi Speranza, economist at BNP Paribas, expects the first-quarter GDP growth will eventually net out slightly better than the initial estimate, but will still show a decline of 0.1%.

But a bigger slump isn't out of the question. Many economists don't have incredibly high levels of confidence in their forecasts at this point, given the wide-ranging impacts of President Donald Trump's whipsawing trade policies.

"Consumers, businesses, and governments are continuing to grapple with an operating environment that is very challenging to navigate and where rules, processes, and previously well-established structures are in a state of constant flux," writes Anthony Saglimbene, chief market strategist at Ameriprise.

The economists at Goldman Sachs expect Thursday's update to show a further contraction. The team forecasts the BEA will lower their estimates for inventory investment, residential investment, and net export gains, leading to a decline of 0.7% in first-quarter GDP.

The first quarter's slowdown was largely due to a massive surge in imports, as companies rushed to purchase goods and inputs ahead of expected higher tariffs. Import growth counts as a subtraction in the BEA's calculation of GDP.

Typically the dent to GDP created by increased imports is canceled out by a similar gain in inventory — which is additive to GDP growth. Yet the climb in imports wasn't fully matched by a rise in inventories or consumer spending — creating a substantial drag on calculations for economic growth.

Shipments of pharmaceuticals and related products, for example, drove much of the first quarter's import growth. But those import gains corresponded with only a meager rise in the inventories of drug wholesalers. Pantheon Macroeconomics' team estimates that the gap was equivalent to more than two percentage points of headline growth.

The Pantheon team anticipates the agency will raise the estimate of wholesale inventories, which would lead the headline estimate to swing from a decline to about 0.5% growth. However, they warned the odds of no change or even a downward revision couldn't be ruled out.

One of the key areas of optimism in the first-quarter data released last month was real final sales to private domestic purchasers — which serves as a measure of how many domestically produced goods are sold. The metric, widely considered to be the engine of the economy, posted a gain of 3% in the first quarter, compared with an increase of 2.9% in the fourth quarter. Economists will be watching this figure closely to determine how much of a tailwind it could provide for the second quarter.

Write to Megan Leonhardt at megan.leonhardt@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: DBV Technologies Price Target Announced at $7.25/Share by Goldman Sachs
Sienna Senior Living Inc. Announces Results From Annual and Special Meeting
data.description:
MARKHAM, Ontario, May 29, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Sienna Senior Living Inc. (“Sienna” or the “Company”) is pleased to announce that it has received shareholder approval in respect of all resolutions to be voted upon at its Annual and Special Meeting of Shareholders held on May 28, 2025 (the “Meeting”). In particular, all nominees listed in the Company’s management information circular dated April 8, 2025 were elected as directors of the Company. The proxy voting results for each nominee are as follows:

NomineeVotes ForVotes Withheld
No.%No.%
Barbara Bellissimo38,120,86699.05367,0130.95
Paul Boniferro38,176,53499.19311,3450.81
Dr. Gina Parvaneh Cody34,888,36290.653,599,5179.35

Shelly Jamieson37,707,46997.97780,4092.03
Brian Johnston38,057,86798.88430,0121.12
Stephen Sender34,859,43590.573,628,4449.43

Proxy voting results for all matters voted on at the Meeting will be filed on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

About Sienna Senior Living Inc.

Sienna Senior Living Inc. offers a full range of senior living options, including independent living, assisted living and memory care under its Aspira retirement brand, long-term care, and specialized programs and services. Sienna’s approximately 14,500 employees are passionate about cultivating happiness in daily life. For more information, please visit www.siennaliving.ca.

For further information, please contact:

Nancy Webb

Executive Vice President, Corporate Affairs and Marketing

data.title: Did You Lose Money on West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. (WST)? Levi & Korsinsky Urges Investors to Act Before July 7, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 29, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/west-pharmaceutical-services-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=150723&wire=1&utm_campaign=28

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between February 16, 2023 and February 12, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (a) despite claiming strong visibility into customer demand and attributing headwinds to temporary COVID-related product destocking, West was in fact experiencing significant and ongoing destocking across its high-margin HVP portfolio; (b) West's SmartDose device, which was purportedly positioned as a high-margin growth product, was highly dilutive to the Company's profit margins due to operational inefficiencies; (c) these margin pressures created the risk of costly restructuring activities, including the Company's exit from continuous glucose monitoring contracts with longstanding customers; and (d) as a result of the foregoing, defendants' positive statements about the Company's business, operations, and prospects were materially false and/or misleading or lacked a reasonable basis.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/west-pharmaceutical-services-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=150723&wire=1&utm_campaign=28 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

CONTACT:

Levi & Korsinsky, LLP

Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

Ed Korsinsky, Esq.

33 Whitehall Street, 17th Floor

New York, NY 10004

jlevi@levikorsinsky.com

https://zlk.com/

data.title: Cattle Turn Higher as Screwworm Fears Fester — Market Talk
data.description: Live cattle futures on the CME are up 0.6%, with trading tentative while the market monitors the New World screwworm infection situation. "Experts on the New World screwworm believe it isn't if the fly enters the U.S., but when," says ADM Investor Services in a note. Monitoring stations are being added at the U.S.-Mexico border, the firm says, but a solution to the outbreak of the parasite remains slow to develop. Lean hog futures are down 0.3% in early trading. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Canadian Stocks Edge Lower
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite Index edged down to hover at the 26,240 mark on Thursday after closing at a record high the prior session, underperforming US counterparts due to the lower exposure for the tech sector on the Toronto Exchange.

A Federal US court blocked the batch of tariffs pushed by President Trump on his international economic emergency measures, alleviating the 25% tariffs on Canada that were in place since April.

Still, the benchmark equity index remained lower as the heavyweight financial sector trimmed its recent rally.

RBC sank over 3% after missing earnings estimates for the second fiscal quarter, driving TD Bank, and NBC to trade lower.

CIBC also hovered in the red following its earnings report.

In the meantime, Shopify advanced nearly 1%, tracking the tech sector as Nvidia's uplifting results supported tech across the globe.

data.title: EMX Royalty Options Rights for Prospective Gold Property in Nevada to Canadian Miner
data.description:
EMX Royalty said Thursday it has optioned developed rights for its Trench Gold project property in Nevada to Canadian junior miner Ridgeline Mineral in exchange for cash and stock payments over the next five years along with other financial considerations.

The Trench Project is an oxide gold system in central Nevada that was previously explored by several groups during the 1990s using surface trenching and limited drilling. The property also contains widespread anomalous gold mineralization, with the exploration work by EMX identifying prospective targets that appear to have been largely overlooked by prior explorations, the company said.

Under terms of the companies' agreement, Ridgeline said it can acquire 100% of the project area by issuing 250,000 shares, making a $650,000 options payment and completing $500,000 of exploration activities over the next five years. EMX is also eligible for 3% net smelter returns, yearly royalty payments and selected milestone payments should Ridgeline exercise its option.

EMX shares were 1% lower in recent early trading.

data.title: Ibovespa Fluctuates on Tax Uncertainty, Strong Jobs Data
data.description:
The Ibovespa traded small gains and losses to remain near 139,000 on Thursday, underperforming American stocks after Finance Minister Fernando Haddad reiterated that there is no alternative to raising the Financial Transactions Tax, despite market backlash and a partial reversal.

He warned of a potential public sector shutdown if the measure is fully annulled.

Airline Azul fell 70% year-on-year and may revisit historic lows after filing for Chapter 15 bankruptcy protection in the US Market.

On the data front, the unemployment rate was sharply below expectations, aligned with the CAGED beat from yesterday to indicate a strong labor market.

Also, a US court ruling blocked tariff measures from President Trump to remove current 10% tariffs on Brazil and other economies.

Meat producers were split, with JBS rising 0.7% and Minerva down 1%.

Petrobras and Embraer opened lower, the latter edging down.

Banks also diverged, with Itaú Unibanco inching higher and Banco do Brasil down firmly.

data.title: Fidelis Partnership Middle East GWP totals $88 million in unit's first year of operations
data.description:
(The Insurer) - The Fidelis Partnership (TFP) on Wednesday said Fidelis MEASA, its Middle East operation, delivered $88 million in gross written premium (GWP) in its first year.

The contribution helped push the underwriter’s group-wide GWP to $4.6 billion in 2024, the company said. Fidelis MEASA’s performance highlights region’s importance in TFP’s global BRICs+ strategy.

Fidelis MEASA’s executive chairman Matthew Warren said the growth in Abu Dhabi is a testament to how entrepreneurial energy and a relationship-driven strategy can deliver outsized impact.

“With enormous investment and ambition underpinning economic growth across the Middle East, local projects will increasingly depend upon sophisticated risk transfer for their success and viability.”

Youssef Al Kareh, CEO and chief underwriting officer of Fidelis MEASA, said TFP’s nontraditional structure enabled it to bring “unique product offerings and dynamic risk solutions” to regional markets.

The company said the Abu Dhabi office would remain central to its expansion strategy in the region and across other BRICs+ markets.

data.title: Baltic index near two-week peak on higher capesize vessel rates
data.description:
The Baltic Exchange's dry bulk sea freight index, which tracks rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, rose on Thursday on higher rates for the capesize vessel segment.

* The main index , which monitors rates for capesize, panamax and supramax shipping vessels, added 50 points, or 3.8%, to 1,353, its highest in nearly two weeks since May 16.

* The capesize index (.BACI) was up 185 points, or 9.9%, at 2,059, its highest level since May 2.

* Average daily earnings for capesize vessels (.BATCA), which typically transport 150,000-ton cargoes such as iron ore and coal, rose $1,535 to $17,074.

* Iron ore futures snapped a four-day losing streak on Thursday, buoyed by upbeat market sentiment after a U.S. federal court blocked President Donald Trump's tariffs from going into effect.

* "Overall, the Forward Freight Agreements (FFAs) indicate that the market expects rates for panamax and supramax ships to remain largely stable during the rest of 2025, while capesize freight rates could firm up," Filipe Gouveia, shipping analysis manager at BIMCO, said.

* The panamax index (.BPNI) slid 32 points, or 2.7%, to 1,137, its lowest in more than two months since March 11.

* Average daily earnings for panamax vessels (.BPWT), which usually carry 60,000-70,000 tons of coal or grain, receded $288 to $10,235.

* Among smaller vessels, the supramax index (.BSIS) dipped 8 points to 956, a near four-week low.

data.title: SentinelOne Stock Sinks. Why Cybersecurity Is a Flop This Earnings Season. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Elsa Ohlen

SentinelOne stock plummeted on Thursday as the market digests its lowered annual guidance. It was the latest disappointment for the cybersecurity sector as economic concerns look to be causing customer hesitation.

SentinelOne stock was down 13% to $17.15 in premarket trading on Thursday.

The downward move contrasted starkly with futures tracking the Nasdaq 100 which were rallying 2% following strong earnings from leading chip maker Nvidia and a federal trade court striking down President Donald Trump's so-called reciprocal tariffs late Wednesday.

The report shows a continuing trend of softer results from cybersecurity peers, KeyBanc analysts led by Eric Heath said. Uncertain macroeconomic conditions are causing deals to be pushed to the future, according to Heath, who rates the stock Sector Weight without a price target.

Jefferies analyst Joseph Gallo said SentinelOne appears to have been affected more than peers such as Palo Alto Networks, potentially due to a higher proportion of new customers. He has a Buy rating and $23 target price on SentinelOne stock.

D.A. Davidson analysts led by Rudy Kessinger maintained a Neutral rating on SentinelOne shares following the earnings report, noting the lowered guidance. Kessinger lowered his price target on the stock to $17 from $18.

In a call with investors late Wednesday, CEO Tomer Weingarten said SentinelOne saw improved trends in May, expecting growth in the second fiscal quarter to be better compared with the first quarter. "This was mostly isolated to kind of a [first quarter] dynamic," he said, referring to the decline in deals during the period.

Cybersecurity peers Fortinet and Palo Alto Networks also disappointed with their respective earnings earlier this year, sending shares down across the sector. Zscaler is set to report earnings Thursday after the bell while CrowdStrike's next set of results is due on June 3.

SentinelOne lowered its full-year 2026 annual recurring revenue guidance to a range between $996 million and $1.001 billion, down from between $1.007 billion and $1.012 billion previously.

SentinelOne's per-share loss for the quarter ended April 30 came in at 63 cents, compared with a loss of 23 cents in the same quarter a year ago and far below the 24 cents per-share loss expected by analysts, according to FactSet. On an adjusted basis, the company earned 2 cents a share.

Revenue came in at $229 million, largely in line with expectations. Annual recurring revenue was $948 million, slightly below expectations of $952 million.

Coming into Thursday trading, the stock was down 11% so far this year and currently trades with a forward price to earnings ratio of 81.9, a higher valuation than Fortinet, Palo Alto and Zscaler, but lower than CrowdStrike. The average forward earnings ratio for Nasdaq-listed companies is 26.7.

Write to Elsa Ohlen at elsa.ohlen@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Market Chatter: JPMorgan Chase Names Kamal Jabre as M&A Vice Chair to Drive EMEA Expansion
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase named former HSBC dealmaker Kamal Jabre as new M&A vice chair to drive growth in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, Reuters reported Thursday, citing a memo.

JPMorgan also appointed insider Marc Pandraud as M&A vice chair alongside Jabre, the report said.

Pandraud and Jabre will work with JPMorgan's local industry, country and M&A staff for its EMEA expansion, the report added.

An HSBC spokesperson confirmed that Jabre had left the bank, Reuters reported, citing an emailed statement.

The banks didn't immediately respond to requests for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: Al Yamamah Steel Industries Lands SAR167.6 Million Power Line Project
data.description:
Al Yamamah Steel Industries (SASE:1304) secured a contract worth 167.6 million Saudi riyals from Trading & Development Partnership Co. to supply steel towers for an ultra-high-voltage power line in Saudi Arabia.

The one-year project involves the construction of 380-kilovolt ultra-high-voltage line in the country's western region, with the supply phase set to begin in August, the metal products manufacturer said Thursday.

The contract's financial impact will begin to be reflected in the fourth quarter.

data.title: Webus International Exploring $300 Million Non-Equity Financing for XRP Reserve; Shares Up
data.description:
Webus International said Thursday it is exploring a financing strategy of up to $300 million through non-equity financing to establish an XRP (XRP/USD) reserve, aiming to support global payment services while maintaining financial flexibility and protecting shareholder value.

Webus also said it extended its nationwide mobility collaboration with Tongcheng Travel Holdings, an online travel platform in China.

Shares of Webus were up more than 38% in recent early trading.
